name: Message Intent
summary: Protocol defining communication priority (Attention) and structural anatomy across alerts, banners, and dialogs. Tone dimension participates in the unified canonical tone vocabulary shared with Feedback and Validation intents (#1427).

attention.description: Priority level determining how the message interrupts the user

tone.description: Semantic meaning conveyed by the message — the canonical `tone` vocabulary (#1427). Synonyms normalized to one token: success (≡ positive), warning (≡ caution), danger (≡ negative ≡ error). neutral and info stay distinct.

description:
Attention Hierarchy

  • ambient: Low priority. Status bars or helper text.
  • routine: Standard priority. Toasts or inline notes.
  • urgent: High priority. Banners or System Alerts.
  • critical: Blocking priority. Dialogs or Screen Takeovers.

Tone Semantics

tone is part of a unified canonical tone vocabulary (#1427) shared across Message (structural), Feedback (transient), and Validation (input states). Synonyms normalize to one token:

  • neutral: No semantic. Distinct from info.
  • info: Informational. No action required.
  • success (≡ positive): Confirmation. Positive outcome achieved.
  • warning (≡ caution): Advisory. Issue detected but not blocking. User may act.
  • danger (≡ negative ≡ error): Failure. Problem occurred. User action required.
